title: New Zealand passes the Civil Union Act
description: New Zealand's parliament approved civil unions for same-sex couples in 2004. The law offered legal recognition of partnerships but did not include joint adoption rights.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: New Jersey enacts a domestic partnership law
description: The US state of New Jersey passed a limited domestic partnership law in 2004, and it took effect the same year, giving same-sex couples a restricted set of legal protections.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Germany allows step-adoption for registered partners
description: Germany extended step-adoption to registered same-sex partners in 2004, letting a partner legally adopt the other partner's biological child.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Australia writes a same-sex marriage ban into federal law
description: Australia's federal parliament amended the Marriage Act in 2004 to define marriage as a union of a man and a woman, ruling out recognition of same-sex marriages.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Five US states adopt constitutional same-sex marriage bans
description: Voters in Mississippi, Missouri, Montana, Oregon and Utah approved state constitutional amendments in 2004 barring same-sex couples from marrying.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ten US states ban same-sex marriage and civil unions
description: In 2004, Arkansas, Georgia, Kentucky, Louisiana, Michigan, North Dakota, Ohio, Oklahoma, Virginia and Wisconsin adopted constitutional amendments blocking both same-sex marriage and civil unions.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Portugal adds sexual orientation to its discrimination protections
description: Portugal adopted anti-discrimination protections covering sexual orientation in 2004, anchored by a constitutional revision that added the ground to its equality clause.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Indiana, Louisiana and Maine expand discrimination protections
description: In 2004, Indiana added gender identity and Louisiana added sexual orientation to public-sector employment protections, while Maine adopted broader anti-discrimination coverage.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Lithuania equalizes the age of consent
description: Lithuania set a single age of consent regardless of gender in 2004, removing the higher threshold that had applied to same-sex activity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Cape Verde decriminalizes same-sex activity
description: A new penal code took effect in Cape Verde in 2004 without provisions against consensual same-sex relations, ending their criminalization.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: The Marshall Islands decriminalize same-sex activity
description: The Marshall Islands removed criminal penalties for consensual same-sex relations in 2004 as part of a revision of the country's criminal law.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: San Marino decriminalizes same-sex activity
description: San Marino repealed its remaining criminal provision against consensual same-sex relations in 2004.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: The L Word brings an ensemble lesbian cast to television
description: The US series The L Word premiered in 2004 as the first television show built around an ensemble cast of lesbian characters.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First all-transgender performance of The Vagina Monologues
description: Eighteen transgender women performed The Vagina Monologues in 2004 in the production's first all-transgender staging, which added a new monologue about trans women's experiences.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: San Francisco marries Del Martin and Phyllis Lyon
description: Mayor Gavin Newsom directed San Francisco to grant marriage licenses to same-sex couples in February 2004, and longtime activists Del Martin and Phyllis Lyon were the first to marry. California courts later voided the 2004 marriages; the couple wed again in 2008 when the state supreme court opened marriage to same-sex couples.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: New Jersey governor James McGreevey comes out as gay
description: James McGreevey came out in 2004 while serving as governor of New Jersey, the first sitting US state governor to do so openly. He resigned from office shortly afterward.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Bisi Alimi comes out on Nigerian television
description: Activist Bisi Alimi spoke openly about being gay on Nigerian television in 2004, the first person in the country to do so on air.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Luna is the first mainstream young-adult novel with a trans lead
description: Julie Anne Peters' novel Luna appeared in 2004 as the first young-adult book from a major publisher to center a transgender character.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: San Francisco holds its first Trans March
description: The first trans pride march in San Francisco took place in 2004 during the city's pride season.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Karamo Brown becomes the first out gay Black man on reality TV
description: Karamo Brown joined the cast of MTV's The Real World: Philadelphia in 2004, becoming the first openly gay Black man on a US reality series.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Canada legalizes same-sex marriage nationwide
description: Canada's Civil Marriage Act became law in 2005, extending marriage to same-sex couples across the country after most provinces had already opened it through court rulings.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Spain legalizes same-sex marriage with joint adoption
description: Spain opened marriage to same-sex couples in 2005, including full joint adoption rights, and the law took effect the same year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Andorra enacts a partnership law for same-sex couples
description: Andorra passed a registered partnership law in 2005 that took effect the same year, giving same-sex couples legal recognition without joint adoption.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Civil partnerships take effect across the United Kingdom
description: The UK's Civil Partnership Act came into force in December 2005, letting same-sex couples register partnerships in England, Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland. Joint adoption rights followed later in several of the UK's jurisdictions.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Connecticut adopts civil unions
description: Connecticut enacted a civil union law in 2005, the first US state to grant civil unions through legislation without a court mandate, and it took effect the same year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: New Zealand's civil union law takes effect
description: The Civil Union Act came into force in New Zealand in 2005, allowing couples to register civil unions. Joint adoption remained unavailable to same-sex couples.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: California's expanded domestic partnership rights take effect
description: A California law broadening the rights and responsibilities of registered domestic partners came into force in 2005.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Switzerland approves registered partnerships
description: Switzerland passed a federal registered partnership law for same-sex couples in 2005, confirmed by referendum. The law excluded adoption and came into force in 2007.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Slovenia passes a registered partnership law
description: Slovenia's parliament approved a registered partnership law for same-sex couples in 2005, offering limited legal recognition.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Joint adoption opens to same-sex couples in England and Wales
description: Adoption law reform took full effect in England and Wales in 2005, allowing same-sex couples to adopt children jointly.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Latvia writes a same-sex marriage ban into its constitution
description: Latvia amended its constitution in 2005 to define marriage as a union between a man and a woman.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Uganda adopts a constitutional ban on same-sex marriage
description: Uganda amended its constitution in 2005 to prohibit marriage between people of the same sex, adding a marriage ban on top of existing criminal laws against same-sex relations.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Honduras bans same-sex marriage
description: Honduras amended its constitution in 2005 to bar same-sex couples from marrying.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Kansas and Texas ban same-sex marriage and civil unions
description: Voters in the US states of Kansas and Texas approved constitutional amendments in 2005 blocking both same-sex marriage and civil unions.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Illinois and Maine outlaw private-sector LGBTQ discrimination
description: In 2005, the US states of Illinois and Maine extended private-sector discrimination protections to cover both sexual orientation and gender identity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Puerto Rico repeals its sodomy law
description: Puerto Rico removed the sodomy provision from its penal code in 2005, formally decriminalizing consensual same-sex relations in the US territory.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Two gay teenagers are executed in Iran
description: Mahmoud Asgari and Ayaz Marhoni, two teenagers, were publicly hanged in Iran in July 2005. Their executions drew international protests against Iran's death penalty for same-sex acts.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: André Boisclair is elected leader of the Parti Québécois
description: André Boisclair won the leadership of the Parti Québécois in 2005, the first openly gay man elected to head a major North American political party.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Pine City hosts the first rural pride in North America
description: East-Central Minnesota Pride was held in Pine City, Minnesota in 2005, the first LGBT pride event staged in a rural North American community.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Vatican bars men with "deep-seated homosexual tendencies" from priesthood
description: The Roman Catholic Church issued an instruction in 2005 excluding men who present deep-seated homosexual tendencies, or who support gay culture, from entering the priesthood.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: The Simpsons airs a full episode on same-sex marriage
description: In 2005 The Simpsons became the first animated series to devote an entire episode to the topic of same-sex marriage.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First European Transgender Council meets in Vienna
description: Trans activists from across Europe gathered in Vienna in 2005 for the first European Transgender Council meeting, the founding event of the network Transgender Europe.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: San Francisco issues the first human rights report on intersex people
description: The Human Rights Commission of the City and County of San Francisco released a report on the situation of intersex people in 2005, the first human rights body to examine the issue.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jewish Renewal ordains its first openly gay rabbi
description: Eli Cohen was ordained in 2005 as the first openly gay man to become a rabbi within the Jewish Renewal movement.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Andrew Goldstein is drafted as an openly gay pro lacrosse player
description: Goaltender Andrew Goldstein joined Major League Lacrosse's Boston Cannons in 2005, becoming the first openly gay man active in a US professional team sport during his playing career.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: South Africa legalizes same-sex marriage
description: South Africa's Civil Union Act passed and took effect in 2006, making it the first African country to open marriage, with joint adoption, to same-sex couples.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: The Czech Republic enacts registered partnerships
description: The Czech Republic passed a registered partnership law for same-sex couples in 2006, and it came into force the same year. Joint adoption was not included.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Slovenia's partnership registration law takes effect
description: Slovenia's law on registered same-sex partnerships came into force in 2006, a year after its adoption by parliament.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mexico City approves civil unions
description: Mexico City passed a cohabitation partnership law in 2006, the first legal recognition of same-sex couples anywhere in Mexico.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: New Jersey passes a civil union law
description: The US state of New Jersey approved civil unions for same-sex couples in 2006 after a state supreme court ruling required equal treatment of same-sex partners.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: South Australia recognizes same-sex partners
description: The Australian state of South Australia passed a limited partnership recognition law in 2006, extending certain legal rights to same-sex couples.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Israel recognizes same-sex marriages performed abroad
description: Israel's Supreme Court ruled in 2006 that same-sex marriages contracted abroad must be recorded in the population registry, granting recognition to couples married overseas.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tennessee adopts a constitutional same-sex marriage ban
description: Voters in the US state of Tennessee approved a constitutional amendment in 2006 defining marriage as between a man and a woman.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Seven US states ban same-sex marriage and civil unions
description: In 2006, Alabama, Colorado, Idaho, South Carolina, South Dakota, Virginia and Wisconsin approved measures barring same-sex marriage and, in most cases, civil unions as well.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Belgium opens joint adoption to same-sex couples
description: Belgium legalized adoption by same-sex couples in 2006, three years after opening marriage to them.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: The Faroe Islands outlaw discrimination based on sexual orientation
description: The Faroe Islands, a self-governing Danish territory, added sexual orientation to their anti-discrimination law in 2006.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Germany passes the General Equal Treatment Act
description: Germany adopted a comprehensive equal treatment law in 2006 covering discrimination on grounds including sexual orientation and gender identity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: New Zealand extends discrimination protections to gender identity
description: New Zealand confirmed in 2006 that discrimination based on gender identity is unlawful under its human rights framework.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US states broaden LGBTQ discrimination protections in 2006
description: Illinois, New Jersey, Washington and Washington, D.C. broadened employment discrimination protections in 2006, variously adding sexual orientation and gender identity coverage.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Kentucky rescinds discrimination protections for state workers
description: Kentucky's governor removed sexual orientation and gender identity from the state's public employment protections in 2006, voiding an earlier executive order.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Hong Kong strikes down its unequal age of consent
description: Hong Kong courts ruled in 2006 that the higher age of consent applied to sex between men was unconstitutional, equalizing the threshold.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: The Isle of Man equalizes the age of consent
description: The Isle of Man, a British crown dependency, set a uniform age of consent in 2006, removing the higher age that had applied to same-sex activity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Serbia equalizes the age of consent
description: Serbia introduced a single age of consent in 2006, ending the higher threshold for same-sex relations.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Moscow's first pride march is met with violence
description: The first attempt at a pride march in Moscow in 2006 was banned by city authorities and ended in violent attacks on participants.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Zagreb hosts the first regional Eastern European Pride
description: Activists from several countries gathered in Zagreb, Croatia in 2006 for the first pride organized as a regional Eastern European event.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Springfield, Missouri repeals its gay soliciting laws
description: The city of Springfield, Missouri removed its local ordinances against gay soliciting in 2006.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US Senate rejects the Federal Marriage Amendment
description: A proposed US constitutional amendment defining marriage as between a man and a woman failed in the Senate in 2006, falling short of the votes needed to advance.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Montreal hosts the International Conference on LGBT Human Rights
description: The International Conference on LGBT Human Rights convened in Montreal in 2006, producing the Declaration of Montreal on LGBT rights.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
